symptoms.symptom_name: Postural lightheadedness with visual darkening on standing, relieved by sitting/lying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Strictly postural presyncope with visual darkening relieved by sitting is characteristic of orthostatic hypotension.
Purely postural symptoms relieved by lying down are not typical of an arrhythmia.
Alpha-blockers blunt the vasoconstrictor baroreflex; a recent dose increase is a classic iatrogenic precipitant.
A near-collapse on standing demonstrates the functional severity and immediate fall risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Coat-hanger ache across the neck and shoulders after standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A coat-hanger ache from postural neck-muscle hypoperfusion is fairly specific for orthostatic hypotension.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, no palpitations/irregular pulse, no true rotational vertigo

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of palpitations/irregular pulse and non-postural symptoms lowers the likelihood of an arrhythmia.
Presyncope with visual darkening rather than rotational spinning argues against BPPV.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained orthostatic BP drop (>30/15 mmHg) on active-stand testing

investigation_details.expected_result: A sustained fall of >=20/10 mmHg (here >30/15 mmHg) within 3 minutes of standing, confirming orthostatic hypotension
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Orthostatic hypotension is not objectively confirmed and severity is not quantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A sustained postural BP drop on active-stand testing confirms orthostatic hypotension.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Strictly postural presyncope with visual darkening and a coat-hanger ache after a recent alpha-blocker increase indicates iatrogenic orthostatic hypotension, managed by withholding the drug, volume expansion, counter-manoeuvres and fall prevention with prompt review.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies the postural BP drop; a home cuff during symptoms is unreliable.
Screens for an arrhythmia as an alternative cause of presyncope.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Medication review and (if needed) basic bloods (electrolytes, renal function)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reviews the offending alpha-blocker and excludes contributing volume/electrolyte factors.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance for a stable, red-flag-negative postural presyncope
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Unnecessary over-triage; medication review and fall prevention with prompt GP review are appropriate, reserving emergency care for red flags.
